note: ESM-2 t6 (8M params, 320-dim) significantly underperforms BLOSUM62 (180-dim) for peptide-MHC binding prediction. Training plateau at 70%. Likely causes: (1) t6 is too small — general evolutionary features lack the positional specificity of BLOSUM62; (2) Mean pooling over sequence dilutes position-specific signals that are critical for MHC anchor recognition; (3) BLOSUM62 directly captures amino acid substitution probabilities relevant for binding. Next steps: try larger ESM-2 models (t12 480-dim, t30 640-dim) or position-aware pooling.
